summary:
The dental pulp is innervated by the trigeminal nerve, primarily through its mandibular branch (V3). Other cranial nerves such as the facial, glossopharyngeal, or hypoglossal do not provide sensory supply to tooth pulp.  

(Note: This summary strictly adheres to only clinically significant facts related to pulp innervation.)
